name: ptouch-cradle
description: Two-part desktop cradle + label-catch tray for Brother PT-P750W label printer. Owl motif consolidated onto the cradle back panel: rounded-top sculptural panel with gently convex +Y face, intrinsic facial disc recess at ~62% height carrying forward-facing eyes + 3D beak wedge, and ear tufts emerging from the rounded panel top (wider-than-tall, tilted 25° outward, 3D-swept forward). Printer section 86mm → shelf section 108mm transition is a r=8 concave fillet (no chamfer). Tray is a clean utility catch basin (no face) with a 45° scoop on the upper ~14mm of its front wall and an integrated concave finger-grip in the scoop center.

params.cable_slot_style: top-open notch (no bridge), resides entirely within low perimeter wall zone z=0-25mm. Lives on user-back (-Y) face.
params.ear_tuft_silhouette: wider-than-tall, tilted 25° outward, 3D-swept forward 8mm, feathery with concave mid-dip, base blended into rounded panel top
params.back_panel_beak_shape: 3D wedge, hull of flat base polygon on disc surface and small rounded apex point 4mm forward
params.label_drop_note: PT-P750W auto-cuts tape; labels exit at z=64-79mm desk-ref, clear front wall top at z=29mm desk-ref by 35-50mm, drop forward and down into tray.
params.tape_exit_position: lower-middle of front face, confirmed from product photography
params.usb_dc_ports_face: back (assumed, not confirmed in product photos)
params.user_orientation_note: +Y = user-front (tray slides out here; owl face visible from here). -Y = user-back (against-wall, cable notch). Render in user-frame per id/brief.md hero_views.

testPrintCandidates.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 mm W × 94.2 mm D × 21.6 mm H) must slide smoothly into cradle slot with 0.35 mm per-side clearance. If FDM dimensional error skews either part, tray will bind or rattle.

testPrintCandidates.feature: tray scoop angle with integrated finger-grip
testPrintCandidates.reason: Scoop face at 45° from horizontal plus a central concave finger-grip dip (2.5mm × 30mm) prints as threshold overhang. Verify slicer does not add unnecessary support and surface quality reads cleanly on the scoop + grip combined feature.

Ear tufts are swept forward 8mm in +Y as they rise, and tilted 25° outward in X. The forward sweep creates a transient underhang below the apex of the tuft curve. Tree supports required per brief (supports_permitted). Verify tuft silhouette reads feathery and tip does not sag.
2mm sagitta over 86mm chord may read as subtle or as manufacturing defect. Verify via render + first print whether the convexity is perceptible at desk viewing distance.
1.5mm recess at the minimum legibility threshold for FDM. Verify whether the disc reads as a framed recess at desk distance, or whether depth should increase.
Tray walls at 1.6 mm (4 perimeters at 0.4 mm) are above minimum but thin. Verify wall integrity.
